entities.name: SMART FOOD
entities.description: Food items that are good for the consumer, the planet, and the farmer, aimed at transforming food systems and combating malnutrition

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A condition resulting from poor diets and poverty that the Smart Food approach aims to combat

entities.name: POOR DIETS
entities.description: A global issue characterized by lack of dietary diversity and nutritional deficiency

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: An environmental concern that the Smart Food initiative helps adapt to

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A socio-economic condition contributing to malnutrition and poor diets

entities.name: DIABETES
entities.description: A fast-growing lifestyle disease linked to poor diets

entities.name: LAND AND WATER SCARCITY
entities.description: Environmental constraints affecting food production and security

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: One of the first crops selected as a Smart Food to improve dietary diversity and nutritional status

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: One of the first crops selected as a Smart Food to improve dietary diversity and nutritional status

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A staple crop used as a comparison point for the nutrient values of Smart Food products

entities.name: DIETARY DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of food items consumed, which the Smart Food approach seeks to increase

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL STATUS
entities.description: The health state of an individual as determined by nutritional intake and utilization

entities.name: NUTRIENT VALUES
entities.description: The measure of nutritional components in food products

entities.name: BEHAVIOR CHANGE
entities.description: The process of altering dietary habits to improve adoption of Smart Foods

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY FUN-FILLED APPROACH
entities.description: An approach used to create awareness and develop culturally acceptable products for Smart Foods

entities.name: CULTURALLY SENSITIVE PARTICIPATORY AND INCLUSIVE APPROACH
entities.description: A method of introducing Smart Foods that involves local participation and cultural sensitivity, resulting in higher product acceptance

entities.name: DIRECT INTRODUCTION APPROACH
entities.description: A method of introducing Smart Foods by directly presenting new products to consumers

entities.name: SOCIAL MEDIA COMPETITIONS
entities.description: A promotional activity for Smart Foods used in Mali

entities.name: COOKING SHOWS
entities.description: A promotional activity for Smart Foods used in Kenya and India

entities.name: RECIPE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: A promotional activity for Smart Foods involving popular chefs in Paris and London

entities.name: SCHOOL FEEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: A promotional activity for Smart Foods used in Tanzania and India

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL MILLET FESTIVAL
entities.description: A promotional event used to promote millet in Niger

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where Smart Foods are being mainstreamed as staples

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where Smart Foods are being mainstreamed as staples

entities.name: MYANMAR
entities.description: A country where a case study compared two approaches to introduce Smart Foods

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: A country where Smart Foods were promoted through social media competitions

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: A country where Smart Foods were promoted through cooking shows

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where Smart Foods were promoted through cooking shows and school feeding programs

entities.name: PARIS
entities.description: A city where recipe development for Smart Foods took place

entities.name: LONDON
entities.description: A city where recipe development for Smart Foods took place

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: A country where Smart Foods were promoted through school feeding programs

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country that hosted an international millet festival
